info.title: External Promotions Protocol API
info.description:
The External Promotions Protocol API is a set of definitions that allows merchants to integrate their own external promotions engine with the VTEX platform. This is a protocol, which means the merchant must implement the endpoints described in this documentation as a web API (REST) on their own infrastructure.

⚠️ This feature is only available for accounts using Headless Checkout.

VTEX will call these endpoints during the shopping cart calculation and order lifecycle to:

- Calculate promotions and discounts for a given shopping cart.
- Notify the external promotions provider when an order is created or cancelled, reporting which promotions and coupons were applied.

## Promotion sources

Accounts configure promotions with one of these sources:

- **Native** — VTEX promotions only (no external provider).
- **Hybrid** — native VTEX promotions run first, then VTEX calls the external provider with the post-native cart so both engines can apply.

There is no external-only source. When an account integrates an external promotions provider, it always uses the **hybrid** orchestration model.

>⚠️ This feature is in a closed beta testing phase. If you are interested in participating, open a ticket with the [VTEX Support](https://help.vtex.com/en/support) team.

## Index

### External promotions

- `POST` [Calculate promotions](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/external-promotions-protocol-api#post-/calculatePromotion): calculates the promotions and discounts for a given shopping cart.
- `POST` [Notify promotion usage](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/api-reference/external-promotions-protocol-api#post-/notifyUsage): receives a notification when an order is created or cancelled, informing which promotions and coupons were applied.

## Common parameters

| Parameter name | Description | Type |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `{{providerApiEndpoint}}` | External promotions provider endpoint URL. | Server variable. |

post.description:
Calculates the promotions and discounts applicable to a given shopping cart. VTEX calls this endpoint during the cart calculation process, sending the shopping cart data including items, coupon codes, UTM parameters, and sales channel information. The external promotions provider must return the calculated discounts for each item, along with a summary of all promotions considered.

This endpoint supports **hybrid** orchestration (protocol v1.1). When an account combines native VTEX promotions with an external provider, VTEX sends the cart **after** native evaluation: see `items[].price` (post-native per-unit price) and the optional per-line `items[].nativePromotionLayer`. The provider may also return `allPromotions[].qualificationProgress` describing promotions the shopper has **not yet unlocked**.

In hybrid mode, the provider can optionally return `nativePromotionRemovals` to ask VTEX to drop specific applied native promotions (by the ids exposed in `items[].nativePromotionLayer.appliedPromotions[].id`) from the merged result — either cart-wide or for selected lines — instead of only stacking discounts on top of them. Absent or empty `nativePromotionRemovals` is a no-op.

## Authentication

VTEX authenticates to the provider using the credentials configured for the account. By default this is an API key sent in the `X-API-Key` header (HTTP Basic is also supported). The provider should reject unauthenticated requests with `401 Unauthorized`.

schema.description: Request body for the calculate promotions endpoint.

correlationId.description: Unique identifier for this `calculatePromotion` call, stable across client retries of the same cart step. VTEX links it to execution diagnostics; providers should log it and may use it for idempotency. Added in protocol v1.1.

origin.description: Origin of the shopping cart. Possible values are `Marketplace` and `Fulfillment`.

shopperProfileId.description: Unique identifier of the shopper's profile.

salesChannelId.description: Identifier of the sales channel (trade policy) associated with the shopping cart, as configured on VTEX.

couponCodes.description: List of coupon codes added to the shopping cart.

utms.description: UTM tracking parameters associated with the shopping cart.

customFields.description: Arbitrary custom data forwarded from the cart, grouped by app. The outer keys are app identifiers; each value is a map of field name to string value. Field values longer than 128 characters are dropped by VTEX before sending. Added in protocol v1.1.

additionalProperties.description: Custom fields for a single app, as a map of field name to value.

items.description: List of items in the shopping cart.
Shopping cart item to be evaluated for promotions.

id.description: SKU identifier of the item, as configured in the VTEX catalog.

sellerId.description: Identifier of the seller, as configured on VTEX.

quantity.description: Quantity of this item in the shopping cart.

price.description: Per-unit selling price of the item before the external engine applies. In **hybrid** mode this is the per-unit price **after** native VTEX promotions, so the provider can stack on top.

catalogInfo.description: Catalog information associated with this item.

brandId.description: Brand identifier of the item, as configured in the VTEX catalog.

categoryId.description: Category identifier of the item, as configured in the VTEX catalog.

productId.description: Product identifier of the item, as configured in the VTEX catalog.

collectionIds.description: List of collection identifiers this item belongs to, as configured in the VTEX catalog.

ean.description: EAN (barcode) of the item, when VTEX has it. Added in protocol v1.1.

refId.description: Merchant reference id (RefId) of the item, when VTEX has it. Added in protocol v1.1.

paymentInfo.description: List of payment methods applied to this item.

items.description: Payment method information for an item.

id.description: Identifier of the payment method, as configured on VTEX.

value.description: Value paid with this payment method.

nativePromotionLayer.description: Hybrid only. How native VTEX promotions changed this line before the external engine runs. When present, `priceAfterLayer` equals `price`. Added in protocol v1.1.

priceAfterLayer.description: Per-unit price after native promotions (equals `price` in hybrid mode).

appliedPromotions.description: Native promotions applied in this layer only.

discount.description: Per-unit discount applied by this promotion.

type.description: Type of discount applied. Currently, only `Nominal` is supported.

schema.description: Response body for the calculate promotions endpoint.

items.description: List of items with their calculated promotion results.
Item in the calculate promotions response.

id.description: SKU identifier of the item in the shopping cart.

quantity.description: Quantity of the item in the shopping cart.

promotions.description: List of promotions applied to this item.

discount.description: Discount value applied by this promotion.

type.description: Type of discount applied. Currently, only `Nominal` is supported.

originalPrice.description: Original per-unit price of this line before external deals. In hybrid mode, typically the per-unit price after native promotions (the pre-external price the provider augments).

discountedPrice.description: Per-unit price of the item after external promotions are applied.

externalPromotionLayer.description: Hybrid: how the partner (external) promotion layer changed this line. When set, `priceBeforeLayer` matches `originalPrice` and `priceAfterLayer` matches `discountedPrice`. Added in protocol v1.1.

priceBeforeLayer.description: Per-unit price before the partner layer (post-native in hybrid).

priceAfterLayer.description: Per-unit price after the partner layer.

appliedPromotions.description: Partner deals applied in this layer only.

discount.description: Per-unit discount applied by this promotion.

type.description: Type of discount applied. Currently, only `Nominal` is supported.

allPromotions.description: List of all promotions considered for this shopping cart, including those that were not applied.

couponCode.description: Coupon code that triggers this promotion. Returns `null` if no coupon code triggered the promotion.

qualificationProgress.description: For **not-yet-unlocked** promotions, structured qualification data VTEX maps to native teasers ("almost there" messaging). All properties are optional; omit the object (or send `{}`) when there is no structured progress. Added in protocol v1.1; not present in v1.0.

remainingCartWideAmount.description: Monetary amount still needed across the whole cart to meet a cart-wide spend threshold (the remaining gap, not the full threshold).

orderSubtotalMustExceed.description: Cart items-subtotal floor the rule requires (the target threshold).

remainingItemQuantity.description: Item/SKU quantity qualification context: optional aggregate and per-SKU rows.

totalUnitsRemaining.description: Aggregate extra units still needed across the qualifying SKU set.

perSku.description: Per-SKU contribution toward the quantity conditions.

skuId.description: Cart/catalog line id, aligned with items[].id in the same request.

unitsInCart.description: Units of this SKU currently in the cart toward this promotion.

unitsRequiredForPromotion.description: Units of this SKU required by the rule.

unitsRemaining.description: Units of this SKU still needed to satisfy its part of the condition.

minimumRuleQuantity.description: Minimum qualifying units the rule requires (e.g. buy-N / combo thresholds).

contextTotalForQualifyingItems.description: Subtotal value range and item scope for total-value rules.

itemScope.description: Which line items' value participates in the subtotal check.

itemUnitPriceBounds.description: Per-unit price band on a line.

minPrice.description: Minimum per-unit item price for qualification.

maxPrice.description: Maximum per-unit item price for qualification.

skuIdGroups.description: SKUs in scope of the rule. Use `allOf` for a single set; `group1`/`group2` for mix-and-match.

allOf.description: All listed SKUs participate in a single set.

group1.description: First SKU group in a two-group rule.

group2.description: Second SKU group in a two-group rule.

inclusivity.description: Whether the shopper's zip is included (`inclusive`) or excluded (`exclusive`).

paymentMethodIds.description: Payment method ids the shopper must use.

cardBinPrefixRestrictions.description: Card BIN prefix patterns the card number must satisfy.

teaserStyleHints.description: UI grouping hints for the teaser message.

nativePromotionRemovals.description: Optional, hybrid-only. Native VTEX promotions the provider asks VTEX to remove from the merged cart result. Absent or empty is a strict no-op (same as the append-only merge). Reference each promotion by the id exposed in `items[].nativePromotionLayer.appliedPromotions[].id`. Only native promotions exposed via `nativePromotionLayer` (price and gift) are removable; unknown, non-applied, or non-removable ids (shipping, tax, reward) and malformed entries are silently ignored and never fail the calculation. Added in protocol v1.1.

items.description: Request to remove an applied native VTEX promotion from the merged result.

promotionId.description: Native promotion identifier to remove, matching `items[].nativePromotionLayer.appliedPromotions[].id` from the request. Ids that are not applied or not removable are silently ignored.

scope.description: Removal scope, matched case-insensitively. Use `cartWide` to remove the promotion from all lines, or `perLine` to remove it only from the cited `lineItemIds`. When omitted, defaults to `cartWide`. For the same `promotionId`, a `cartWide` entry absorbs any `perLine` entries; repeated `perLine` entries union their `lineItemIds`. Unrecognized values are discarded without failing the response.

lineItemIds.description: Cart item ids (`items[].id`) to strip when `scope` is `perLine`. Required and non-empty for `perLine`; ignored for `cartWide`. Cited lines the promotion did not touch are silently ignored.

items.description: Cart item identifier to strip, aligned with `items[].id` in the same calculate promotions call.

allPromotions.name: 25 cents off on cokes
allPromotions.description: Buy at least 3 cokes and get 25 cents off on each

allPromotions.name: Free shipping over $50
allPromotions.description: Spend $50 or more to unlock free shipping

post.description:
Receives a notification when an order is created or cancelled, informing the external promotions provider which promotions and coupons were applied to the order. This allows the provider to track promotion usage, enforce usage limits, and maintain accurate promotion state.

The optional `correlationId` references the `calculatePromotion` evaluation that led to the order, when VTEX can supply it, for end-to-end tracing. Added in protocol v1.1.

## Authentication

VTEX authenticates to the provider using the credentials configured for the account (API key in the `X-API-Key` header by default; HTTP Basic also supported). The provider should reject unauthenticated requests with `401 Unauthorized`.

schema.description: Request body for the notify usage endpoint.

correlationId.description: When available, the `correlationId` of the `calculatePromotion` evaluation that led to this order, for end-to-end tracing. Added in protocol v1.1.

orderId.description: Identifier of the order triggering this notification.

type.description: Type of notification. `NewOrder` indicates a new order was placed, and `OrderCancellation` indicates an order was cancelled.

promotionUsages.description: List of promotions used in this order.

items.description: Details of a promotion applied to the order.

discount.description: Total discount value given by this promotion.

couponCode.description: Coupon code that triggered this promotion. Returns `null` if no coupon code triggered the promotion.

apiKey.name: X-API-Key
apiKey.description: API key provided by the external promotions provider for authentication.

Basic.description: Basic authentication provided by the external promotions provider.

Content-Type.name: Content-Type
Content-Type.description: Type of the content being sent.

Accept.name: Accept
Accept.description: HTTP Client Negotiation _Accept_ Header. Indicates the types of responses the client can understand.
